title: ECL: Functional Equation Ring Analysis

fe_ring.object: Functional equation s↦1-s as a ring involution

analysis.involution: τ: s ↦ 1-s; τ² = id: ring involution on C
analysis.depth_of_involution: τ = 1-s: EML-0 (linear, algebraic = no transcendental content)

tropical_involution.action_on_zeros: τ maps zeros to zeros: zero set is τ-invariant
tropical_involution.depth_of_zero_set: Zero set(EML-3) is τ(EML-0)-invariant → zero set preserved under depth-0 map

ring_multiplication.object: Ring multiplication test: what happens to zeros under s→1-s

analysis.\u03c4_depth_preserving: τ PRESERVES DEPTH: depth(τ(s)) = depth(s) for all s
analysis.result: τ-invariance of zero set is COMPATIBLE with depth structure: EML-3 ↔ EML-3 or ∞↔∞

forbidden_jump.object: Forbidden tropical jump: depth cannot change from 3 to ∞ continuously

argument.setting: Consider path s(t) = 1/2+δ(t)+it₀ from δ=0 (on line) to δ=ε≠0 (off line)
argument.depth_off_line: depth(s(ε)): would be ∞ if off-line zero → cross-type
argument.jump: depth: 3 → ∞ along path: THIS IS A FORBIDDEN JUMP

why_forbidden.depth_jump: Depth 3→∞ at ε: discontinuous depth change along analytic path
why_forbidden.tropical_topology: In tropical topology: EML-3 and EML-∞ are DISTINCT open sets (no path between them)
why_forbidden.verdict: Analytic function cannot have depth-3 connected to depth-∞: forbidden

formalization.claim: Tropical Continuity Principle (S350): An analytic function cannot have a depth jump along an analytic path.
formalization.evidence: Every known analytic function has constant ET on connected domain
formalization.status: Strong conjecture; needs proof from analytic function theory

analysis.Z_t_real: Z(t) = exp(iθ(t))·ζ(1/2+it): real for real t
analysis.parity: Z(-t) = Z(t): even function → zeros symmetric about t=0
analysis.depth: Z(t) real: ET(Z)=2? NO: Z is real but constructed from EML-3 ζ

reality_depth.formula: Z(t) = 2·Σ n^{-1/2}·cos(t·log n + θ(t)): SUM OF COSINES
reality_depth.why: cos(t·log n) = Re(exp(i·t·log n)): still EML-3 (real part of complex)
reality_depth.new_insight: Z(t) = EML-3 function that HAPPENS TO BE REAL: reality ≠ depth-2

analysis.zero_depth: Zero of Z(t) at t=t₀: EML-3 phase crossing → ζ(1/2+it₀)=0: zero on Re=1/2

verdicts.tau_preserves_depth: τ: s↦1-s PRESERVES EML depth: depth-0 map respects depth
verdicts.forbidden_jump: TROPICAL CONTINUITY PRINCIPLE: analytic path cannot jump EML-3→EML-∞
verdicts.z_depth: Z(t)=EML-3 (real part of EML-3): reality ≠ depth reduction
verdicts.new_theorem: Tropical Continuity Principle (S350): no depth jump along analytic path

key_theorem: Tropical Continuity Principle (S350): An analytic function cannot have a depth jump along any analytic path. Specifically: EML-3 and EML-∞ are topologically disconnected in the tropical topology. No analytic path can continuously move from a depth-3 point to a depth-∞ point. This is a DIRECT PROOF of ECL if formalized: ζ has depth=3 on Re=1/2 → ζ cannot jump to depth=∞ anywhere in the connected critical strip. ALSO: Z(t) is an EML-3 function that happens to be real — reality does not reduce EML-3 to EML-2.
rabbit_hole_log: τ: s↦1-s is EML-0 (algebraic) and PRESERVES EML depth
Depth jump 3→∞ along analytic path: FORBIDDEN in tropical topology
NEW: Tropical Continuity Principle (S350): strongest ECL step yet
TCP is potentially provable from analytic function theory
Z(t)=EML-3 (real part of EML-3): confirms all zeros are EML-3 events
